| MCP:SLwYYI | '''ResNet in PyTorch.
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | For Pre-activation ResNet, see 'preact_resnet.py'.
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | Reference:
| MCP:SLwYYI | [1] Kaiming He, Xiangyu Zhang, Shaoqing Ren, Jian Sun
| MCP:SLwYYI |     Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition. arXiv:1512.03385
| MCP:SLwYYI | '''
| MCP:SLwYYI | import torch
| MCP:SLwYYI | import torch.nn as nn
| MCP:SLwYYI | import torch.nn.functional as F
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | class BasicBlock(nn.Module):
| MCP:SLwYYI |     expansion = 1
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def __init__(self, in_planes, planes, stride=1):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         super(BasicBlock, self).__init__()
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(
| MCP:SLwYYI |             in_planes, planes, kernel_size=3, stride=stride, padding=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(planes, planes, kernel_size=3,
| MCP:SLwYYI |                                stride=1, padding=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.shortcut = nn.Sequential()
| MCP:SLwYYI |         if stride != 1 or in_planes != self.expansion*planes:
| MCP:SLwYYI |             self.shortcut = nn.Sequential(
| MCP:SLwYYI |                 nn.Conv2d(in_planes, self.expansion*planes,
| MCP:SLwYYI |                           kernel_size=1, stride=stride, bias=False),
| MCP:SLwYYI |                 nn.BatchNorm2d(self.expansion*planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI |             )
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def forward(self, x):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.bn2(self.conv2(out))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out += self.shortcut(x)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         return out
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | class Bottleneck(nn.Module):
| MCP:SLwYYI |     expansion = 4
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def __init__(self, in_planes, planes, stride=1):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         super(Bottleneck, self).__init__()
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_planes, planes, kernel_size=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(planes, planes, kernel_size=3,
| MCP:SLwYYI |                                stride=stride, padding=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(planes, self.expansion *
| MCP:SLwYYI |                                planes, kernel_size=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn3 = nn.BatchNorm2d(self.expansion*planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.shortcut = nn.Sequential()
| MCP:SLwYYI |         if stride != 1 or in_planes != self.expansion*planes:
| MCP:SLwYYI |             self.shortcut = nn.Sequential(
| MCP:SLwYYI |                 nn.Conv2d(in_planes, self.expansion*planes,
| MCP:SLwYYI |                           kernel_size=1, stride=stride, bias=False),
| MCP:SLwYYI |                 nn.BatchNorm2d(self.expansion*planes)
| MCP:SLwYYI |             )
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def forward(self, x):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(out)))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.bn3(self.conv3(out))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out += self.shortcut(x)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         return out
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | class ResNet(nn.Module):
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def __init__(self, block, num_blocks, num_classes=10):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         super(ResNet, self).__init__()
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.in_planes = 64
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 64, kernel_size=3,
| MCP:SLwYYI |                                stride=1, padding=1, bias=False)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.layer1 = self._make_layer(block, 64, num_blocks[0], stride=1)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.layer2 = self._make_layer(block, 128, num_blocks[1], stride=2)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.layer3 = self._make_layer(block, 256, num_blocks[2], stride=2)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.layer4 = self._make_layer(block, 512, num_blocks[3], stride=2)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         self.linear = nn.Linear(512*block.expansion, num_classes)
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def _make_layer(self, block, planes, num_blocks, stride):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         strides = [stride] + [1]*(num_blocks-1)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         layers = []
| MCP:SLwYYI |         for stride in strides:
| MCP:SLwYYI |             layers.append(block(self.in_planes, planes, stride))
| MCP:SLwYYI |             self.in_planes = planes * block.expansion
| MCP:SLwYYI |         return nn.Sequential(*layers)
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |     def forward(self, x):
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.layer1(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.layer2(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.layer3(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.layer4(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = F.avg_pool2d(out, 4)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = out.view(out.size(0), -1)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         out = self.linear(out)
| MCP:SLwYYI |         return out
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | def ResNet18():
| MCP:SLwYYI |     return ResNet(BasicBlock, [2, 2, 2, 2])
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | def ResNet34():
| MCP:SLwYYI |     return ResNet(BasicBlock, [3, 4, 6, 3])
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | def ResNet50():
| MCP:SLwYYI |     return ResNet(Bottleneck, [3, 4, 6, 3])
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | def ResNet101():
| MCP:SLwYYI |     return ResNet(Bottleneck, [3, 4, 23, 3])
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI | def ResNet152():
| MCP:SLwYYI |     return ResNet(Bottleneck, [3, 8, 36, 3])
| MCP:SLwYYI | 
| MCP:SLwYYI |

| MCP:SLwYYI |     net = ResNet18()
